perturbation.original_text: 11. GOVERNING LAW AND JU RISDICTION This Agreement and any non -contractual obligations arising out of or in connection with it shall be governed by the laws of The Netherlands and the competent court in The Hague, The Netherlands shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute which may arise from or in connection with it.
perturbation.changed_text:
10.4 This Agreement may be executed in counterpart, each of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument and the parties may execute this Agreement by signing in counterpart. A facsimile or pdf signature of any party shall be deemed to be an original.

11. GOVERNING LAW AND JU RISDICTION This Agreement and any non -contractual obligations arising out of or in connection with it shall be governed by the laws of The Netherlands and the competent court in The Hague may hear disputes, but the parties agree to first attempt mediation in a location of mutual agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The governing law clause is altered to suggest mediation is a pre-requisite, and to cast doubt on the exclusive jurisdiction of The Hague. This weakens the enforceability of the jurisdiction clause.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Dutch Code of Civil Procedure Article 1020
perturbation.law_citation: Article 1020 Dutch Code of Civil Procedure
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 1020 grants civil courts the authority to issue preliminary relief in arbitration cases. By suggesting mediation first, the modified text potentially delays access to such relief, hindering the court's ability to act swiftly. Moreover, the shift from 'exclusive jurisdiction' to a non-committal phrasing after 'The Hague' casts doubt on the parties' initial agreement regarding court jurisdiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 This Agreement shall come into effect (i) until parties sign the underlying documentation in relation to the Project, or (ii) in the event parties do not sign any such documentation , on the date of this Agreement and shall continue to be in full force and effect for a period of 2 years.
perturbation.changed_text:
9.1 Neither party shall disclose or announce the Purpose to another person without the prior written consent of the other party (such consent not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed) except to the extent that such disclosure or announcement is required as specified in Clause 9.2

8.1 This Agreement shall come into effect (i) until parties sign the underlying documentation in relation to the Project, or (ii) in the event parties do not sign any such documentation , on the date of this Agreement and shall continue to be in full force and effect until either party deems it no longer necessary.
perturbation.explanation: The clause defining the term and termination is modified to allow unilateral termination. This undermines the initial 2-year commitment, making the confidentiality agreement easily voidable.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Dutch Contract Law - Reasonableness and Fairness (redelijkheid en billijkheid)
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 6:248 states that a contract has the effects agreed upon by the parties, but also those that, according to the nature of the agreement, arise from law, custom or the requirements of reasonableness and fairness. Allowing one party to unilaterally terminate a confidentiality agreement at will, especially after a period of information exchange, may be deemed unreasonable and unfair, thereby undermining the legally binding nature of the original term.

perturbation.original_text: 4. DISCLOSURE OF THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORM ATION 4.1 Each party may disclose the Confidential Information of the other party: 4.1.1 with the prior written consent of the other party; 4.1.2 to its employees, professional advisors, authorised representatives , potential underwriters or sub contractors to the extent that disclosure is necessary or desirable for the Purpose; 4.1.3 where disclosure is required by law, by a court of competent jurisdictio n or by another appropriate regulatory body provided that the Receiving Party shall give to the Disclosing Party not less than two business days’ notice of such disclosure; or
perturbation.changed_text:
6.  FURTHER UNDERTAKI NGS 6.1 No right o r licence is granted to either party in relation to the other party's Confidential Information except as expressly set out in this Agreement .

4. DISCLOSURE OF THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORM ATION 4.1 Each party may disclose the Confidential Information of the other party: 4.1.1 with or without the prior written consent of the other party; 4.1.2 to its employees, professional advisors, authorised representatives , potential underwriters or sub contractors to the extent that disclosure is necessary or desirable for any purpose; 4.1.3 where disclosure is required by law, by a court of competent jurisdictio n or by another appropriate regulatory body with no need to provide notice.
perturbation.explanation: The disclosure clause is changed to remove the requirement for prior written consent and any specific purpose. Also, the text removes the obligation to provide notice. This severely weakens the protection of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Dutch Trade Secrets Act (Wet bescherming bedrijfsgeheimen)
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 5 allows a trade secret holder to seek an injunction against someone who unlawfully obtained, used, or disclosed a trade secret. By removing the restrictions on disclosure, the modified text essentially allows for the free disclosure of what would otherwise be protected trade secrets, making it more difficult for the disclosing party to seek legal remedies under the Trade Secrets Act.

perturbation.original_text: 3. KEEPING CONFIDENT IAL INFORMATION C ONFIDENTIAL Each party shall in relation to the Confidential Information of the other party exercise no lesser security measures and degree of care than those which it applies to its own confidential information and which it warrants as providing adequate protection against any unauthorised disclosure, copying or use.
perturbation.changed_text:
10.  GENERAL 10.1 The failure to exercise or delay i n exercising a right or remedy provided by this Agreement or by law does not constitute a waiver of the right or remedy or a waiver of other rights or remedies. No single or partial exercise of a right or remedy provided by this Agreement or by law prevent s a further exercise of the right or remedy or the exercise of another right or remedy.

3. KEEPING CONFIDENT IAL INFORMATION C ONFIDENTIAL Each party shall in relation to the Confidential Information of the other party exercise security measures and degree of care that are reasonable.
perturbation.explanation: The clause requiring parties to use at least the same security measures for the other party's confidential information as they use for their own is weakened to a 'reasonable' standard. This introduces ambiguity and makes it more difficult to prove a breach of contract.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Dutch Contract Law - Duty of Care (zorgplicht)
perturbation.law_citation: Unwritten Principle of Dutch Contract Law - Duty of Care (zorgplicht)
perturbation.law_explanation: While not codified in a single article, Dutch law includes an unwritten duty of care (zorgplicht) in contractual relationships. This means that parties must act as a 'good contract party' and take reasonable steps to protect each other's interests. By reducing the standard of care to simply 'reasonable', the modification makes it harder to prove that a party breached their duty of care, as what constitutes 'reasonable' is open to interpretation, potentially falling below the standard the parties would employ with their own confidential information. The removed text is more specific and aligns better with the standard of care the law requires in these situations.

perturbation.original_text: 7. PUBLICATION OF INFOR MATION FMO may be obliged to disclose through publication on its website, and other (social) media, certain details of the Project and FMO’s participation in a potential financing (no commercially sensitive or Confidential I nformation ), for a period of at least 30 days, in advance of contracting . FMO and the Company will enter into separate and more detailed arrangement on disclosure prior to contracting of such financing.
perturbation.changed_text:
1.  INTERPRETATION 1.1 In this Agreement: "Confidential Information " means any information of a confidential nature relating to the Disclosing Party or a member of its Group disclosed (whether before or after the date of this Agreement and whether in writing, verbally or by any other means and whether directly or indirectly) by the Disclosing Party or by another person on behalf of the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party or to another person on behal f of the Receiving Party including, without limitation, any information relating to the Purpose or to the Disclosing Party's products, operations, processes, plans or intentions, product information, know how, design rights, trade secrets, market opportuni ties and business affairs;

7. PUBLICATION OF INFOR MATION FMO may be obliged to disclose through publication on its website, and other (social) media, all details of the Project and FMO’s participation in a potential financing , immediately without any notice or contracting.
perturbation.explanation: The clause about FMO's publication rights is modified to remove the protection for commercially sensitive or confidential information, to remove the advance notice, and to remove any mention of separate arrangements. This allows for the immediate and unrestricted release of project details, potentially violating the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Dutch Unfair Competition Act (Wet oneerlijke handelspraktijken)
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 6:193b defines unfair trading practices. While primarily aimed at consumer protection, the principle can extend to B2B relationships where one party is placed at a significant disadvantage. By unilaterally disclosing all project details without notice, FMO could potentially damage the Company's competitive position, constituting an unfair trading practice by revealing data that is trade-sensitive, particularly if it impairs the Company's position and decision-making capabilities as a result of the disclosure.
